name: Bhai Didar Singh Nangal
body: Bhai Didar Singh Ji was one of the many gems produced by Prof. Darshan Singh Komal’s school of music. The other distinguished students of Komal Ji include Bhai Dharam Singh Zakhmi, Bhai Beant Singh Bijli, Bhai Chanan Singh Majboor, Bhai Gian Singh Surjit and many more. All of them were very proficient in Gurbani as well as the tradition of Gurmat Sangeet. This was a continuation of the Rababi legacy of Bhai Sain Ditta Ji who taught Prof. Darshan Singh Komal. Coming back to Bhai Didar Singh Ji, he was one of the most talented of Komal Ji’s students and eventually became the heir to his legacy. There is a very in depth article written by Sardar Harjap Singh Aujla regarding Bhai Didar Singh Ji which is a must read.

I’d just like to say a few words that were shared with me by Sardar Harcharan Singh Ji who hosted the jatha of Bhai Didar Singh Ji at his home for a month during his tour in Malaysia in the 70s. Fondly referring to him as Master Didar Singh Ji, he told me that many people used to come from far to learn from Bhai Didar Singh Ji during his stay. Even Giani Mahinder Singh Khalsa used to cycle to his home every day for lessons. Sardar Harcharan Singh Ji had a spool player during that time and used to record many visiting jathas. He also recorded 4 reels of Bhai Didar Singh Ji. Unfortunately, those reels were taken by someone during the 1980s and he never got it back. If anyone has a copy of these reels or any other recordings of Bhai Didar Singh Ji, please contact us.

One incident that Sardar Harcharan Singh Ji narrated to me was that a few years after Bhai Didar Singh Ji’s visit to Malaysia, Harcharan Singh Ji went for a holiday to India with his wife and visited Anandpur Sahib. When he checked into the hotel and got to his room, both husband and wife started chatting. Then a voice came from the room next door asking, “Dr. Sahib is that you?” Coincidentally, Bhai Didar Singh Ji was in the next room. Such were Bhai Didar Singh Ji’s finely tuned ears, that he recognised his voice in an instant. They shared a wonderful time together during their stay there and it was the last they met. Sadly, Bhai Didar Singh Ji passed away not too long after.
